host_terminal_report: HOST SQUARE ERROR,GIMBAL STARTUP ESCAPE GUARD

physical_observation.description_zh: 一启动车辆向左，随后停车；用户没有移动小车，黑线仍在传感器下，第6/7路指示灯仍亮。

guard_evidence: At 2280, 2300 and 2320 ms the valid mask was 96 and line_error was 23, so the GUARD4 host threshold stopped the run. No sample lost the line. The firmware 12 degree heading guard and the 160 mm/s target-delta guard did not trigger.
diagnosis: The vehicle was already recovering when the host stopped it: yaw fell from 7.1 degrees at 2040 ms to 5.4 degrees at 2400 ms, and the measured left wheel had begun overtaking the right wheel. GUARD4 incorrectly treated the still-valid 6/7-sensor mask as an immediate escape. A second bottleneck was that the encoder-derived mechanical trim had grown to roughly 31 mm/s while mechanical trim and grayscale steering shared the same 35 mm/s total envelope, leaving almost no independent line-recovery authority.
rollback_confirmation: STOP was confirmed and the saved GIMBAL GSTART=10, LINEKP=6750, LINEKD=2000 and V4 turn values were restored in RAM.
corrective_action: GUARD5 classifies valid outer line as RECOVER rather than STOP, lowers common speed to 80 mm/s during recovery, separates mechanical, IMU-heading and grayscale steering budgets, raises the bounded recovery turn envelope to 60 mm/s, adds per-wheel adaptive breakaway PWM, uses a 1.0 second GIMBAL launch ramp, and restores learned GIMBAL wheel-PI load compensation after each turn. The host hard guard now reserves immediate stopping for sustained invalid line, the outermost hard error, or very large yaw.
